commit.message: fix(LegalLinks): expose crawlable Privacy and Terms links for OAuth branding verification (#217) Google OAuth branding verification flagged `https://wingdex.app` because the homepage did not expose a detectable link to the Privacy Policy. The legal links were rendered as button-driven SPA navigation instead of real anchors, and the same pattern also affected the Terms-to-Privacy cross-link. - **What changed** - Replaced footer `Privacy` and `Terms` controls with real anchor links: - `/#privacy` - `/#terms` - Preserved in-app navigation by letting the browser follow those anchors and updating the hash router on `hashchange`. - Updated the Terms page's `Privacy Policy` reference to use the same real URL instead of a non-resolving fragment. - Kept standard link behavior intact so modified clicks like Cmd/Ctrl-click still work normally. - **Router behavior** - Added `hashchange` handling so anchor-based legal navigation updates the rendered page even when navigation comes from plain `href` changes rather than tab clicks. - **Why this matters** - The homepage now contains explicit legal page URLs that crawlers and OAuth branding review can detect. - Privacy and Terms remain navigable both as direct URLs and through normal SPA interactions. - The footer links now retain native browser semantics for opening in a new tab or window. ```tsx Privacy ``` - **Screenshot** - OAuth verification issue example: `https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7be9e8e-27bf-4b54-a344-27dc3a111541` > [!WARNING] > > --- ✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for you](https://github.com/jlian/wingdex/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot) — coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for your repo. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: jlian <2320572+jlian@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use aggregate_current for SIMILAR total type in percentile calc (#6750)

* cleanup: remove unreachable defensive error logs in aggregate graph item validation

* fix: use aggregate_current for SIMILAR total type in percentile calc

When computing Nth percentile for aggregate graphs with "Total Similar
Data Sources" total type, the code incorrectly used aggregate_sum (which
sums all data sources together) instead of aggregate_current (which
filters by matching data_source_name). This caused in/out 95th
percentile HRULEs to display identical values on aggregate graphs.

Split the SIMILAR and ALL total type cases in three locations:
- aggregate_graphs_insert_graph_items() text_format substitution
- aggregate_handle_ptile_type() for COMMENT items
- aggregate_handle_ptile_type() for HRULE items

SIMILAR now uses aggregate_current / aggregate_current_peak, which
compute percentiles per data source name across member graphs. ALL
continues to use aggregate_sum / aggregate_sum_peak.

Fixes #6470

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

* test: verify SIMILAR total type uses per-data-source percentile calculation

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

* chore: remove unintended api_aggregate delta from PR scope

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

* fix: correct function name in test docblock

The text_format replacement is in aggregate_graphs_insert_graph_items(),
not aggregate_graph_items_save().

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

* fix: separate SIMILAR from ALL in text_format and pparts replacements

AGGREGATE_TOTAL_TYPE_SIMILAR was grouped with ALL in three locations in
lib/api_aggregate.php, causing SIMILAR graphs to use aggregate_sum /
aggregate_sum_peak (summed totals) instead of aggregate_current /
aggregate_current_peak (per-source percentiles). Split each if block
into separate ALL and SIMILAR elseif branches.

lib/api_aggregate.php is the intentional target of this fix. This is not
cleanup or drift from another branch -- the three buggy if conditions
were always here and are the root cause of issue #6470.

Fixes #6470

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

- Fix typo: $next_parent_it -> $next_parent_id in get_graph_parent()
- Update version_to_decimal() @return docblock from 'integer' to 'int'
- Restore api_aggregate.php accidentally removed by prior pollution commit
- Fix pre-existing lib/dsstats.php alignment and PHPStan bootstrap order

commit.message:
Add convex hull vs halfspace narrowphase collision

Implement phys_convex_hull_vs_halfspace() for detecting collisions between
convex hull shapes and infinite halfspace planes (ground/walls). Transforms
hull vertices to world space, finds deepest penetrating vertices, and returns
up to 4 contacts with correct normals.

Add dispatch for CONVEX × HALFSPACE in all three narrowphase paths:
- narrowphase.c (serial)
- narrowphase_par.c (parallel)
- collision_fused_par.c (fused pipeline)

Fix two solver issues that prevented effective ground collision response:
- Set default baumgarte to 0.2 (was 0.0) so contacts provide position
  correction velocity, not just velocity-level prevention
- Cap baumgarte bias at 10 m/s to prevent explosive impulses from deep
  penetration (common when bodies fall through before detection)

Include ghost bodies in island merging so skeleton joints and ground contacts
end up in the same solver island. Without this, joint pseudo-velocity
corrections could fight ground contact corrections across separate islands.

Remove temporary debug output from tick_parallel.c, integrate_par.c,
and collision_fused_par.c.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(fuzzer): remove query-qmp-schema; normalize obj IDs; add query-memory-size-summary

Three changes to improve corpus quality and exec throughput:

1. Remove query-qmp-schema from all op paths.
   - Returned 230 KB of constant JSON per call; under 8-worker load the I/O
     latency routinely exceeded the 800 ms timeout, generating false-positive
     timeout objectives and polluting the crashes directory.
   - Replaced with query-iothreads (op-0 QUERY_COMMANDS) and
     query-memory-size-summary (op-11 branch) -- both small responses that
     probe different QEMU subsystems.

2. Add 'obj to DIRECT_PATTERNS in normalize_qemu_stdout().
   - object-del error messages embed the object ID: object 'obj0' not found
   - Without normalization each distinct ID produced a unique corpus hash,
     inflating the corpus with semantically equivalent entries.

3. Replace 'mem with 'obj in DIRECT_PATTERNS.
   - Fuzzer IDs are dev<N> (devices) and obj<N> (objects), not mem<N>.
     Previous fix normalised the wrong prefix.

Result: exec/sec improved from ~16 to ~22 per worker (+37%); corpus
add-rate started at 75% at 39 s (vs 100% before) and is expected to
plateau lower as the behavioral space saturates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix title page PDF page label for pdfpc compatibility

When title slides use freeze-slide-counter: true, the slide counter
stays at 0. This caused counter(page).update(0) which Typst encodes
without /St in the PDF (defaulting to label "1" per PDF spec), while
the first regular slide also has /St 1. Both pages got the same PDF
page label "1", causing pdfpc to mismatch pages and lose presenter
notes for the last overlay of the first slide.

Fix: use calc.max(1, slide-counter) for both the page counter update
and the pdfpc LogicalSlide metadata, ensuring the title page gets
a valid, explicit /St 1 in the PDF and a consistent label "1" in
the pdfpc file.

Add litebox_packager tool for self-contained ELF packaging (#704)

## Summary

Add `litebox_packager`, a CLI tool that packages Linux ELF programs into
self-contained `.tar` bundles for execution under LiteBox. The tool
automates the full workflow: dependency discovery, syscall rewriting,
and archive creation.

## Changes

### New: `litebox_packager` crate
- Discovers shared library dependencies via `ldd`
- Rewrites all ELF binaries using `litebox_syscall_rewriter`
- Produces a `.tar` with all files at their expected absolute paths
(copies instead of symlinks, since the tar RO filesystem does not
support them)
- Embeds `litebox_rtld_audit.so` at `lib/litebox_rtld_audit.so` (built
at compile time, always without DEBUG)
- Supports `--include HOST:TAR` for extra files, `--no-rewrite` to skip
specific files, and `--verbose` for diagnostics
- Fails on missing shared library dependencies with an actionable error
message

### Runner improvements (`litebox_runner_linux_userland`)
- Add `--program-from-tar` flag to load the program binary from the tar
archive instead of the host filesystem
- Runtime validation that `--program-from-tar` requires
`--interception-backend=rewriter`
- Change default interception backend from `seccomp` to `rewriter`
- Improved help text: program path docs clarify host vs tar behavior
- Actionable hint when program not found on host but `--initial-files`
is set
- Fix `ancestor_modes_and_users.last().unwrap()` panic proper error with
`ok_or_else`

### Windows runner simplification
(`litebox_runner_linux_on_windows_userland`)
- Simplify to always load programs from a tar archive (`--initial-files`
is now required)
- Remove `--program-from-tar`, `--rewrite-syscalls`, and `--insert-file`
flags (no longer needed since all programs must come from tar)
- Remove host filesystem program loading path (ancestor dir creation,
Windows-to-Unix path conversion)
- Always inject `LD_AUDIT=/lib/litebox_rtld_audit.so` via
`set_load_filter` so rewritten binaries find the audit library
automatically
- Remove unused `litebox_syscall_rewriter` and `windows-sys`
dependencies
- Update dynamic test to include hooked binary in tar and use
tar-internal path
- Only print platform system information in debug builds

### Build fixes (`litebox_runner_linux_userland/build.rs`)
- `env_remove("DEBUG")` in release builds to prevent inheriting stale
env
- Force rebuild of `litebox_rtld_audit.so` on profile change

## Usage

```bash
# Package a program (Linux)
litebox_packager /usr/bin/ls -o ls.tar

# Run on Linux
litebox-runner-linux-userland -Z \
  --initial-files ls.tar \
  --program-from-tar \
  /usr/bin/ls [args...]

# Run on Windows
litebox_runner_linux_on_windows_userland.exe -Z \
  --initial-files ls.tar \
  /bin/ls [args...]
```

## Testing

- Manually verified with `ls` packaged and run end-to-end on both Linux
and Windows.
- Windows runner tests pass (static and dynamic linked programs).

commit.message:
fix: blockquote alignment, table column widths, and image loading

Blockquote alignment:
  Replace ui.horizontal() wrapper with scope_builder + explicit rect
  positioning. This eliminates spurious item_spacing.y that caused
  inconsistent vertical gaps between consecutive blockquotes, and
  ensures the bar paints against the correct outer response rect.

Table column widths:
  Rewrite compute_table_col_widths to use content-first approach:
  compute natural widths from char count (not byte length), then
  only normalise when content fits the available space. When natural
  widths exceed the budget, keep them and signal horizontal scroll.
  This fixes text wrapping in wide tables (e.g. verification.md §6.3)
  where the old normalisation forced all columns into usable width,
  making horizontal scroll unreachable.

  Also fixes: CJK column width estimation (chars not bytes), and
  single-column cap now considers row content length.

Image loading:
  Enable 'http' and 'file' features on egui_extras so that the
  EhttpLoader and FileLoader are registered. Without these, images
  from https:// URLs (all bundled doc images) and file:// paths
  (relative images in on-disk documents) silently failed to load.

commit.message:
fix(fuzzer): normalize devNNN/memNNN IDs in QMP error messages

Device and object IDs are echoed verbatim in QEMU error responses:
  Device 'dev42' not found
  object 'mem99' not found

With 256 possible numeric suffixes per ID, each distinct b1/b2 argument byte
produced a unique stdout hash for the same logical outcome, keeping the
corpus add-rate at 100% indefinitely.

Add 'dev and 'mem to DIRECT_PATTERNS with a digit-presence guard so
'device' and 'developer' are not incorrectly normalised.  Device IDs
now consistently hash as 'dev0' / 'mem0' across all argument values.

This reduced the corpus add-rate from 100% to ~65% at 24 s and is
expected to plateau well below 50% as the behavioral space saturates.

sequences immediately following a fixed prefix (no colon/space separator),
normalising '#net141:' → '#net0:' across runs.
